## [03:00 - 06:00]

that's not going to work if they're not certain that this is the right thing, now is the right time. It's not going to work. So in that case, we got to handle the uncertainty. And if we can't handle that, then we just delay instead of follow up. And the reoffer is a strategy that's great for those situations like that. I mean, you should always do the reoffer. But don't give them the payment plans. Because then you can know, hey, in seven days, 10 days, I can reoffer, get them back on, send them a bunch of resources in the meantime. They reoffers the bait to get them back on the call. And I can actually reclose on a two-pay. So when I say this reoffer strategy, what I'm not advising you to do is if your program's 9,800, I'm not advising you to only pitch 9,800. And then they can basically f off if they can't do it. No, if it's a financial thing, you want to give them the payment plans. But when it's uncertainty, meaning it doesn't matter what payment plans you give them, they're not going to buy because they're not certain that this is the right thing and now is the right time to use this to get to where they want to be, then you want to withhold those payment plans because that's negotiating leverage in the future. That's bait to be able to get them back on calls. So very key distinction here that you want to do. ## [06:00 - 08:20]

last time offered a three-pay. If you offered a three-pay, maybe offer a four-pay, even that's a little bit crazy. If you can handle it, do it. If you offer a one-pay, then offer a two-pay. And then at the end of the day, the reason why they're going to join guys isn't because, oh, now they get a three-pay and sort of a two-pay. So it's like, OK, yeah, it's been three months. What's changed? Nothing. OK, well, obviously nothing's it. Probably nothing for the next three months is going to change. And then the last thing I'll say here is every single time you have a prospect that doesn't close, you should basically add them to your hot list with a specific date to reoffer every single time.
